common.metaDescription: Buy, sell, and retire verified carbon credits via Solana smart contracts

hero.subtitle: Buy, sell, and retire verified carbon credits through Solana smart contracts

problem.label: Problem
problem.headline: Kazakhstan ranks in the world's top 10 by GDP carbon intensity
problem.p1: 349 million tonnes of CO₂ per year. 17.5 tonnes per person — almost 3× the OECD average. The carbon credit market has existed since 2013 (KZ ETS) but is effectively dead: $1 per tonne versus $91 in the EU, only 135 participants, spot only, no derivatives.
problem.p2: The government's target: $50 per tonne by 2030 (×50 growth). The World Bank allocated $4.8M to develop the market. Yet banks and financial institutions are barred from trading on Caspy Exchange — by law only industrial emitters may participate. Transparency is zero. Double counting is the norm.

investors.title: Investors
investors.desc: Buy shares of green projects in tenge. Earn dividends from carbon-credit sales. Credit price is $1 today → target $50 by 2030. Potential ROI: ×50. Platform fee: 1.5%.

polluters.title: Emitters
polluters.desc: KazMunaiGas, ArcelorMittal, coal-fired power plants — 135 enterprises must offset their emissions. Transparent purchase and retirement of credits with proof on-chain.

greenProjects.title: Green projects
greenProjects.desc: Solar plants, wind farms, reforestation — funded directly by investors. Verification and all calculations are transparent.

how.label: How it works
how.step1Desc: The owner of a solar plant or wind farm uploads documents. The certificate hash is recorded on-chain — it can't be forged or changed. An independent verifier confirms the project.
how.step2Desc: You pick a project and buy shares with KZTE (1 KZTE = 1 tenge). Funds go to the project's secured vault. Share tokens land in your wallet — proof of your investment.
how.step3Desc: Factories and power plants buy carbon credits from the project. Revenue is automatically distributed to investors pro rata. Claim dividends with one click. Up to $25,000/year on a 10% share at $50/t.
how.step4Desc: When a company offsets its emissions, the carbon tokens are burned forever. The blockchain keeps the record: who retired, how many tonnes, when, and why. A credit can never be used twice — it simply no longer exists.

whySolana.label: Why Solana
whySolana.transparencyDesc: Every operation is visible on-chain. Anyone can verify: how many credits were issued, who bought, who retired.
whySolana.speedDesc: A transaction takes 0.4 seconds and costs less than a tenge. Compared to Ethereum: minutes and thousands of tenge.
whySolana.integrityDesc: Document hash, credit retirement, dividends — all written forever. Can't be changed retroactively.

calculator.label: Carbon calculator
calculator.subtitle: Find out how much CO₂ you produce and offset it via ZhasylKZ. Data is based on emission factors for Kazakhstan.
calculator.methodologyText: Electricity factor — 0.636 kg CO₂/kWh (KZ coal generation). Car — 0.21 kg/km (average gasoline). Aviation — 0.255 kg/passenger-km (ICAO). Gas — 2.0 kg/m³ (natural gas).
calculator.treesHint: needed to absorb in a year

retire.title: Retire credits
retire.subtitle: Retire carbon credits forever. Every retirement creates immutable on-chain proof. Double counting is impossible.
retire.counterLabel: tonnes of CO₂ retired on the platform
retire.connectToLoad: Connect your wallet to load projects
retire.noProjectsBlockchain: No projects on the blockchain. Run the setup-devnet script.
retire.buyHint: Buy Carbon Tokens of project "{name}" for subsequent retirement
retire.selectToRetire: Pick a project to retire credits

steps.step1Desc: Choose a project to retire from
steps.step2Desc: How many tonnes of CO₂ and why

why.immutableDesc: RetireRecord PDA: who, how much, when, why
why.verifiableDesc: anyone can check via Solana Explorer

marketplace.title: Marketplace
marketplace.subtitle: Secondary market for tokenized carbon credit shares

features.p2pDesc: List shares for sale and buy directly from other investors with on-chain escrow.
features.orderbookDesc: Browse all active listings with prices, volumes, and trade history.
features.pricingDesc: Market pricing via competition between buyers and sellers.

marketplace.noListingsHintConnected: Listings will appear when investors put their shares up for sale
marketplace.noListingsHintDisconnected: Connect your wallet to view listings

project.realEquipmentDesc: Generation data is streamed in real time from the inverter via the SOLARMAN API. This is not a simulation — live monitoring of real equipment on the university campus.

invest.description: Enter the number of shares to buy. Price per share: {price} ₸

toasts.notInitialized: Program not initialized. Check your wallet connection.

step1.label: Problem
step1.headline: Kazakhstan ranks in the world's top 10 by GDP carbon intensity
step1.deadMarket: The carbon credit market has existed since 2013 (KZ ETS) but is effectively dead: $1 per tonne versus $91 in the EU. Only 135 participants, spot only, no derivatives. Banks and financial institutions are barred from trading.
step1.personalText: I study renewable energy at the Akhmet Yassawi International Kazakh-Turkish University in Turkestan. There's a real 25 kW solar plant on campus — we connected it to the platform via the SOLARMAN API. The carbon-credit topic isn't abstract for us — we see clean energy generated every day, and we want to give a tool to monetize that contribution via blockchain.

step2.label: Solution
step2.headline: ZhasylKZ — the first marketplace of tokenized carbon credits in Central Asia

step3.label: Real projects
step3.headline: 4 verified projects on devnet
step3.subtitle: Data is based on real green projects in Kazakhstan. On-chain with mock fallback.
step3.liveTitle: Yassawi University Solar Plant — our real project
step3.liveDesc: A 25 kW solar plant on the university campus in Turkestan. Generation data is streamed in real time via the SOLARMAN API — not a simulation, but live monitoring of real equipment.

step4.label: Try it yourself
step4.headline: Investing with KZTE
step4.step1Desc: Click the wallet button in the top right. Network — Devnet.
step4.step2Desc: Go to Portfolio and tap "Get KZTE" — 100,000 ₸ to your wallet.
step4.step3Desc: Open any project → "Invest" → enter the number of shares → confirm. Platform fee — 1.5%.
step4.step4Desc: Share tokens (sYSV, sBRB, sAMT) appear in Phantom. With price growing to $50/t — up to ×50 ROI.

step5.label: Passive income
step5.headline: Dividends from credit sales
step5.howStep1: An emitter (KazMunaiGas, ArcelorMittal) buys carbon credits from the project for KZTE.
step5.howStep2_part2: — KZTE goes into the vault, updating
step5.howStep3: The investor opens Portfolio and clicks "Claim" — taking their share of the dividends.
step5.exampleTitle: Example: 5,000 t CO₂ project, your share 10%
step5.exampleNote: Gas is paid by the investor, not the owner → scalable. Double-claim is impossible via last_claimed.

step6.label: Killer feature
step6.headline: Retire — burn credits forever
step6.step1Desc: Emitter purchases the project's carbon credits
step6.step2Desc: Tokens are destroyed forever — removed from circulation
step6.step3Desc: Immutable on-chain proof: who, how much, when, why
step6.step4Desc: Mint NFT with metadata — visible in Phantom, downloadable SVG
step6.whyImportantText: A burned token doesn't exist. Double counting is impossible. RetireRecord PDA — immutable proof for auditors. Platform fee for retirement — 1%. Anyone can verify on Solana Explorer.

step7.label: Architecture and summary
step7.headline: A full DeFi product on Solana

security.vault: Vault PDA — sole authority over KZTE ATA

step7.summaryFooter: Solana — fast and cheap. Kazakhstan — first in Central Asia. TAM at $50/t — $17.5B.
